channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Ontario's premier says Trump's tariffs would be a disaster for US markets
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — The premier of Canada's most populous province said Tuesday President-elect Donald Trump's plan to impose sweeping 25% tariffs on all Canadian products would be a "disaster" that would hurt U.S. stock markets.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Japanese space startup aborts 2nd satellite launch attempt minutes after liftoff
item.description: A Japanese space startup said its second attempt to launch a rocket carrying satellites into orbit had been aborted minutes after liftoff Wednesday, nine months after the company’s first launch attempt ended in an explosion.
item.dc:creator: Mari Yamaguchi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel plans an extended occupation of Syrian buffer zone
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Israeli troops will occupy a buffer zone inside Syria for the foreseeable future.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Rescuers dig for survivors of Vanuatu earthquake that killed 14 people and injured more than 200
item.description: A magnitude 7.3 earthquake that struck off Vanuatu killed at least 14 people, injured hundreds more and caused widespread damage across the South Pacific island nation, officials said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Graham-Mclay, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Global demand spurring Indonesia's mining boom comes at a cost for many communities
item.description: KABAENA, Indonesia (AP) — The crystal blue waters that once surrounded Kabaena are murky brown now, and the octopi and colorful fish that locals used to catch nearby to eat and sell have fled. The lush seaweed they used to harvest is gone.
item.dc:creator: YUSUF WAHIL and VICTORIA MILKO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Authorities impose overnight curfew in cyclone-ravaged Mayotte as France rushes in aid
item.description: SAINT-DENIS, Reunion (AP) — French authorities on Tuesday announced an overnight curfew in Mayotte as they sought to stabilize the island territory in the aftermath of Cyclone Chido, the most intense storm to hit the Indian Ocean archipelago in 90 years.
item.dc:creator: ADRIENNE SURPRENANT and TOM NOUVIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Deadly violence in Nigeria linked to breakup of United Methodist Church over LGBTQ policies
item.description: A religious schism has turned deadly in Nigeria, with a church member fatally shot and two young children killed as homes were set ablaze, according to United Methodist News Service.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Brazilian Santa swaps sleigh for Jet Ski to deliver toys — and joy — to disabled children in Rio
item.description: Dozens of children with disability gathered on Rio de Janeiro’s Copacabana beach on Tuesday to receive presents — and joy — from the white-bearded man, a joint effort by the city’s firefighters and nonprofit associations.
item.dc:creator: Lucas Dumphreys, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Russian opposition figure says fears of his mother's poisoning in Berlin proved false
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — A prominent Russian opposition politician freed as part of the largest East-West civilian prisoner swap since the Cold War said Tuesday that fears of his mother's poisoning in Berlin have proven false. Vladimir Kara-Murza, a dual Russian-U.K.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Van Hollen, Graham threaten sanctions on Turkey to achieve ceasefire with Syrian Kurdish allies
item.description: Sens. Chris Van Hollen (D-Md.) and Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.) are threatening sanctions against Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan unless Ankara forces Syrian opposition fighters into a ceasefire with U.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Netanyahu says Israel will occupy border territory in Syria for now
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Tuesday that his forces will for now occupy territory they had seized over the border in Syria.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: The killing of a Russian general is one of several that Moscow blames on Ukraine
item.description: Ukraine’s Security Service, or SBU, has claimed responsibility for killing Lt. General Igor Kirillov, head of the military’s nuclear, biological and chemical protection forces, in a bombing Tuesday in Moscow.
item.dc:creator: KATIE MARIE DAVIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 2 charged in deadly drone attack on US soldiers in Jordan
item.description: The U.S. on Monday charged two Iranian officials in a scheme that contributed to a deadly January drone attack that killed three American service members in Jordan. The Department of Justice (DOJ) charged Mahdi Mohammad Sadeghi, 42, who is a U.S.-Iranian national from Natick, Mass.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: DHS overhauls H-1B visa program
item.description: The Biden administration announced a rule Tuesday clarifying who can apply for an H-1B work visa, a key program to attract international talent that’s been criticized as too complicated and susceptible to abuse.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, The Hill

item.title: Battle erupts over South Korean court that will determine the fate of impeached Yoon
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s acting leader vowed Tuesday to convey to the world that things are back to normal following parliament’s impeachment of conservative President Yoon Suk Yeol, as rival parties squabble the appointments of court justices who will determine whether to unseat or r
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG and HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Australian state proposes ban on protests at places of worship to fight rising antisemitism
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — An Australian state government on Tuesday proposed new restrictions on protesters in response to rising antisemitism, including a ban on demonstrations outside places of worship.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: German Chancellor Olaf Scholz loses no-confidence vote, election called for early next year
item.description: In November, Scholz's government collapsed after the Free Democrat Party quit over the country's debt. An early election to replace Scholz will occur on Feb. 23 following Monday's no-confidence vote.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Assad paints himself as 'custodian' to Syria as picture unfolds on collapse of Damascus
item.description: Former Syrian President Bashar al-Assad broke his silence since fleeing the nation following the Islamist rebel takeover and looked to portray himself only ever as a "custodian" of Syria.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FIFA apologizes to Ukraine soccer body for Crimea map error at 2026 World Cup draw
item.description: “While the incident was unintentional, we sincerely regret any concern it may have cause,” FIFA’s chief member association officer wrote in a letter .
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Cartel-run clandestine fentanyl labs increasingly difficult to track
item.description: Mexican drug cartels are driving an opioid epidemic, flooding the United States with increasingly potent synthetic drugs.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli hostages' families hold Central Park rally, call on Biden, Trump to bring loved ones home
item.description: Family members of Israeli hostages met in New York City on Sunday to call for President Biden and President-elect Trump to bring them home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korea’s parliament votes to impeach President Yoon Suk Yeol over his martial law order
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s parliament on Saturday impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol over his stunning and short-lived martial law decree, a move that ended days of political paralysis but set up an intense debate over Yoon's fate, as jubilant crowds roared to celebrate another defiant m
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ex-soccer player Kavelashvili becomes Georgia's president in a blow to country's EU aspirations
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — Former soccer player Mikheil Kavelashvili became president of Georgia on Saturday, as the ruling party tightened its grip in what the opposition calls a blow to the country’s EU aspirations and a victory for former imperial ruler Russia.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIKO MEGRELIDZE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Chinese phone hack victims haven't been notified: Report
item.description: AT&T and Verizon have reportedly not informed some hacking victims that they were specifically targeted.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Woman arrested smuggling pounds of meth wrapped as a Christmas gift
item.description: Customs officers arrested a traveler who investigators say tried to smuggle pounds of methamphetamine disguised as a Christmas gift into New Zealand last weekend.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, NewsNation

item.title: Woman arrested for attempting to smuggle 22 pounds of meth wrapped as Christmas gifts in carry-on bag
item.description: A 29-year-old woman was arrested after she allegedly attempted to smuggle methamphetamine wrapped as Christmas presents in her carry-on bag.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Zealand scientists suspect specimen of world’s rarest whale died from head injuries
item.description: After completing the dissection of the first complete specimen of the world's rarest whale, scientists suspect the 16-foot spade-toothed whale died from head injuries.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: American freed from Syrian prison after Assad's overthrow taken out of country by US military
item.description: Travis Timmerman, an American who was freed by Syrian rebels this week after the overthrow of former President Bashar al-Assad, has been flown out of the country by the U.S. military.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran has new drone ship in Persian Gulf
item.description: Iran has a new ship with the capability of launching drones, according to satellite images reviewed by Maxar Technologies that were shared with The Hill. The new drone ship, called Shahid Bagheri, is anchored offshore of the Iranian naval port of Bandar Abbas in the Persian Gulf, according to Maxar.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, NewsNation
